proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months versus non-stage IV.
Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Older age (age_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
Node-positive disease (node_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Each unit increase in ECOG PS reduces PFS by ~1.18 months; very large negative effect (p<1e-300).
Stage IV disease reduces PFS by 1.54 months on average (p<1e-300).
Brain mets reduce PFS by 1.05 months (p~1.8e-179).
Older age associated with LONGER PFS: +0.176 months per year (p<1e-300). Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis (positive, not negative).
Node positivity not associated with PFS (beta=-0.024, p=0.29).

proposed_hypotheses.text: ER-positive tumors (er_positive=1) are associated with longer pfs_months than ER-negative.
PR-positive tumors (pr_positive=1) are associated with longer pfs_months than PR-negative.
HER2-positive tumors (her2_positive=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months than HER2-negative.
HER2-low tumors (her2_low=1) are associated with longer pfs_months than HER2-zero.
BRCA1 mutation (brca1_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
BRCA2 mutation (brca2_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
PIK3CA mutation (pik3ca_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
TP53 mutation (tp53_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher Ki-67 percentage (ki67_pct) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Larger tumor size (tumor_size_cm) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ER+ patients have +0.57 months longer PFS (p~2.2e-121).
PR+ patients have +0.36 months longer PFS (p~8.1e-55).
HER2+ patients have shorter PFS (-0.46 months, p~1.2e-56).
HER2-low associated with modestly longer PFS (+0.11 months, p~7.4e-7).
BRCA1 mutation not associated with PFS (beta=-0.025, p=0.73).
BRCA2 mutation marginally associated with shorter PFS (beta=-0.13, p=0.078, NS).
PIK3CA mutation associated with shorter PFS (-0.56 months, p~2.4e-126).
TP53 mutation has no detectable main effect on PFS (beta=0.007, p=0.76).
Each percentage point of Ki-67 reduces PFS by 0.019 months (p~3e-93).
Tumor size has no association with PFS (beta=0.0006, p=0.93).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (unconditional).
Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (unconditional).
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (unconditional).
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (unconditional).
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (unconditional).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (unconditional).

analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen main effect not significant (beta=0.043, p=0.077).
Palbociclib main effect strongly positive: +1.10 months PFS (p<1e-300).
Trastuzumab main effect mildly NEGATIVE (beta=-0.06, p=0.030); direction opposite hypothesis.
Olaparib unconditional effect not significant (beta=-0.036, p=0.33).
Sacituzumab govitecan no main effect (beta=-0.007, p=0.84).
Pembrolizumab unconditional effect mildly NEGATIVE (beta=-0.074, p=0.017); direction opposite hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between her2_positive and treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months: HER2+ patients benefit more from trastuzumab than HER2- patients.
There is a positive interaction between er_positive and treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months: ER+ patients benefit more from tamoxifen than ER- patients.
There is a positive interaction between brca_any (brca1_mutation OR brca2_mutation) and treatment_olaparib on pfs_months: BRCA-mutated patients benefit more from olaparib than BRCA-WT.
There is a positive interaction between er_positive and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months: ER+ patients benefit more from palbociclib than ER- patients.

analyses.result_summary: No detectable HER2 x trastuzumab interaction (beta=-0.028, p=0.69). Trastuzumab does NOT differentially benefit HER2+ patients in this dataset; in fact stratified means show slightly LOWER PFS in HER2+ on trastuzumab (4.25) vs off (4.33).
No significant ER x tamoxifen interaction (beta=-0.058, p=0.27). Tamoxifen benefit not differential by ER status.
Positive BRCA x olaparib interaction in univariate model: beta=+0.40 months, p=0.021. BRCA-mutated patients gain ~0.35 months on olaparib while BRCA-WT do not benefit. Marginal significance.
VERY large positive ER x palbociclib interaction: beta=+1.59 months, p~9e-229. Palbociclib offers no benefit in ER- (mean 4.30 vs 4.29) but huge benefit in ER+ (4.31 vs 5.89).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Triple-negative breast cancer (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-TNBC.
There is a positive interaction between TNBC status and treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months: TNBC patients benefit more from sacituzumab govitecan than non-TNBC.
There is a positive interaction between TNBC status and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months: TNBC patients benefit more from pembrolizumab than non-TNBC.

analyses.result_summary: TNBC associated with shorter PFS (beta=-0.49, p~4.8e-72). 21% of cohort is TNBC.
No significant TNBC x sacituzumab interaction (beta=-0.10, p=0.25). Sacituzumab does NOT show differential benefit in TNBC in this cohort, contrary to the canonical clinical mechanism.

Marginal positive TNBC x pembrolizumab interaction (beta=+0.19, p=0.015). Pembrolizumab has slightly negative effect in non-TNBC and roughly neutral in TNBC; net interaction is positive but small magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher serum albumin (albumin_g_dl) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher CRP (crp_mg_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Lower hemoglobin (hemoglobin_g_dl) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher alkaline phosphatase (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher serum calcium (calcium_mg_dl) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Each g/dL of albumin adds 0.50 months PFS (p~9.4e-112). Strong positive prognostic marker.
Higher LDH associated with shorter PFS (beta=-5.1e-4 per U/L, p=2.4e-4).
CRP not significantly associated with PFS (beta=-9e-4, p=0.46).
NLR not significantly associated with PFS (beta=0.003, p=0.54).
Hemoglobin not associated with PFS (beta=-0.006, p=0.30).
Higher alkaline phosphatase associated with shorter PFS (beta=-6.7e-4, p=3.0e-3).
Higher calcium associated with shorter PFS (beta=-0.064, p=0.004).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater 6-month weight loss percentage (weight_loss_pct_6mo) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
There is a negative interaction between ecog_ps and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months: palbociclib benefit is smaller in patients with worse ECOG.
There is a negative interaction between stage_iv and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months: palbociclib benefit is smaller in stage IV patients.

analyses.result_summary: Each percentage point of 6-mo weight loss reduces PFS by 0.075 months (p~4e-148). Strong negative prognostic marker.
No significant ECOG x palbociclib interaction (beta=0.009, p=0.77). Palbociclib effect does not depend on performance status.
No significant stage IV x palbociclib interaction (beta=0.006, p=0.90).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Diabetes mellitus (diabetes_mellitus=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Heart failure (heart_failure=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Chronic kidney disease (chronic_kidney_disease=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Prior malignancy (prior_malignancy=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Depression/anxiety diagnosis (depression_anxiety_diagnosis=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Diabetes not associated with PFS (beta=0.022, p=0.41).
Heart failure not associated with PFS (beta=0.016, p=0.67).
CKD not associated with PFS (beta=0.016, p=0.64).
Prior malignancy not associated with PFS (beta=-0.047, p=0.17).
Depression/anxiety not associated with PFS (beta=-0.030, p=0.30).

proposed_hypotheses.text: snp_rs1045642 (ABCB1 C3435T) is associated with pfs_months (any direction).
snp_rs1065852 (CYP2D6 *4) is associated with pfs_months.
snp_rs1799853 (CYP2C9 *2) is associated with pfs_months.
snp_rs429358 (APOE) is associated with pfs_months.
snp_rs7412 (APOE) is associated with pfs_months.
snp_rs1801133 (MTHFR C677T) is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No association (beta=-0.029, p=0.20).
No association (beta=0.034, p=0.33).
No association (beta=-0.022, p=0.55).
No association (beta=-0.034, p=0.28).
No association (beta=0.004, p=0.91).
No association (beta=0.028, p=0.24).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue grade (fatigue_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher pain numeric rating (pain_nrs) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher dyspnea grade (dyspnea_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cough grade (cough_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher appetite loss grade (appetite_loss_grade) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No association (beta=0.0005, p=0.96).
No association (beta=-0.002, p=0.72).
No association (beta=0.010, p=0.30).
No association (beta=-0.009, p=0.38).
No association (beta=-0.002, p=0.86).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race/ethnicity (race_ethnicity) is associated with pfs_months (non-white categories differ from white).
Insurance type (medicaid or uninsured) is associated with shorter pfs_months relative to private insurance.
Rural residence (rural_residence=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-rural.
Greater pack-year smoking history (smoking_pack_years) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
More years of education (education_years) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No race/ethnicity category differs significantly from white (asian beta=+0.056 p=0.24; black +0.037 p=0.29; hispanic +0.028 p=0.38; other +0.083 p=0.30).
No insurance category differs significantly from private (medicaid beta=+0.021 p=0.54; medicare +0.007 p=0.77; uninsured +0.048 p=0.41).
No association (beta=0.027, p=0.34).
No association (beta=-3e-4, p=0.48).
No association (beta=-0.003, p=0.39); direction opposite hypothesis but NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model adjusting for age, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, ER, HER2, PIK3CA, Ki-67, albumin, weight loss, the er_positive x treatment_palbociclib interaction remains a strong positive predictor of pfs_months.

In a multivariable model, age_years remains positively associated with pfs_months even after adjusting for performance status, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, biomarkers (i.e., the unexpected positive age effect is not confounded).
In the multivariable model, the brca1_mutation x treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation x treatment_olaparib interactions remain positive after adjusting for confounders.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + er_positive + her2_positive + pik3ca_mutation + ki67_pct + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_palbociclib*er_positive + treatment_olaparib*brca1_mutation + treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: treatment_palbociclib:er_positive coefficient = +1.559 months (t=70.1, p<1e-300). Interaction is robust to adjustment for prognostic covariates.

Adjusted age coefficient = +0.176 months/year (t=359.7, p<1e-300), virtually identical to univariate. Counterintuitive positive age effect is NOT explained by ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, or biomarkers.

treatment_olaparib:brca1_mutation beta=+0.017 (p=0.87); treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation beta=-0.068 (p=0.50). Univariate combined BRCA-any signal does NOT survive when separated and adjusted, suggesting it was a chance finding rather than a robust predictive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative interaction between pik3ca_mutation and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months: PIK3CA-mutated patients receive less benefit from palbociclib than PIK3CA-WT.
There is an interaction between pik3ca_mutation and treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between pik3ca_mutation and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between pik3ca_mutation and treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Very strong negative PIK3CA x palbociclib interaction: beta=-1.62, p~4e-255. PIK3CA mutation essentially abrogates palbociclib's PFS benefit.
No interaction (beta=-0.004, p=0.94).
No interaction (beta=0.053, p=0.42).
No interaction (beta=0.065, p=0.40).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Liver metastases (liver_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Bone metastases (bone_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Adrenal metastases (adrenal_mets=1) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Pleural effusion (pleural_effusion=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Pericardial effusion (pericardial_effusion=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No association (beta=0.018, p=0.56).
No association (beta=0.038, p=0.17).
No association (beta=-0.006, p=0.86).
No association (beta=-0.034, p=0.28).
No association (beta=0.021, p=0.72).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between postmenopausal status (postmenopausal=1) and treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months: postmenopausal patients have differential tamoxifen benefit vs premenopausal.

analyses.result_summary: No significant postmenopausal x tamoxifen interaction (beta=-0.043, p=0.39). Postmenopausal main effect also NS (beta=-0.010, p=0.70).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between her2_amplification and treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months (HER2-amplified patients gain from trastuzumab).
HER2 amplification (her2_amplification=1) main effect is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No significant her2_amplification x trastuzumab interaction (beta=0.039, p=0.81). Trastuzumab does not show specific PFS benefit even in HER2-amplified patients.
HER2 amplification has no detectable main effect (beta=+0.053, p=0.48). HER2 amplification is rare (~3%) and largely uncoupled from her2_positive in this cohort (only 275 are both).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Prior chemotherapy (prior_chemotherapy=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Prior radiation (prior_radiation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Prior surgery (prior_surgery=1) is associated with longer pfs_months (resection of disease).
More prior lines of therapy (prior_lines_of_therapy) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
More years since diagnosis (years_since_diagnosis) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No association (beta=0.002, p=0.94).
Marginal positive direction (beta=+0.045, p=0.064, NS).
Prior surgery weakly associated with longer PFS (beta=+0.066, p=0.005), consistent with hypothesized direction.
No association (beta=0.009, p=0.34).
No association (beta=-0.003, p=0.63).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between hormone receptor positivity (hr_pos = er_positive=1 OR pr_positive=1) and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months: HR+ patients benefit substantially more from palbociclib than HR- patients.

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive HR+ x palbociclib interaction (beta=+1.51, p~1.4e-184). Palbociclib increases mean PFS in HR+ from 4.31 to 5.79 months (n=12968 on tx, 24198 off) while showing essentially no change in HR- (4.32 vs 4.29, n=4460 on, 8374 off).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a negative three-way interaction between er_positive, pik3ca_mutation, and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months: the large palbociclib benefit seen in ER+/PIK3CA-WT is abrogated in ER+/PIK3CA-MUT, while ER- patients have no benefit regardless of PIK3CA status.
Within the ER+/PIK3CA-WT subgroup, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by approximately 2 months versus no palbociclib.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction beta=-2.43, t=-24.2, p~1e-128. Subgroup means: ER+/PIK3CA-WT off palbo 4.30 (n=14754) vs on palbo 6.68 (n=8034), gain +2.38 mo. ER+/PIK3CA-MUT off 4.33 vs on 4.36, no benefit. ER- subgroup unchanged regardless of PIK3CA. Confirms PIK3CA as a strong negative predictive biomarker for palbociclib in ER+ disease.
Within ER+/PIK3CA-WT, mean PFS off palbociclib is 4.30 (n=14754) vs on palbociclib 6.68 (n=8034). Treatment effect ~+2.4 months, consistent with hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between age_years and pfs_months is robust to adjustment for ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, and key biomarkers (i.e., it is not confounded).

Age_years is essentially uncorrelated with the major prognostic covariates (|r|<0.02), suggesting the unexpected positive age effect reflects a primary association with pfs_months in this cohort rather than confounding.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + er_positive + her2_positive + pik3ca_mutation + ki67_pct', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coefficient = +0.1758 months/year (p<1e-300), virtually identical to the unadjusted +0.1759. Direction is robust and confirmed.

All correlations |r|<0.01: ecog -0.004, stage 0.004, brain -0.008, albumin 0.003, weight_loss 0.004. Age is essentially independent of standard prognostics in this synthetic cohort, supporting that the age-PFS association is primary, not confounded.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between snp_rs1065852 (CYP2D6 *4) and treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months (pharmacogenomic; CYP2D6 poor metabolizers should derive less tamoxifen benefit).
There is an interaction between snp_rs1045642 (ABCB1) and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between snp_rs1799853 (CYP2C9) and treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between snp_rs4244285 (CYP2C19) and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months.
There is an interaction between snp_rs1800629 (TNF promoter) and treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No interaction (beta=-0.002, p=0.97).
No interaction (beta=-0.050, p=0.28).
No interaction (beta=0.045, p=0.58).
No interaction (beta=0.018, p=0.81).
No interaction (beta=-7e-4, p=0.99).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between her2_low and treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months (HER2-low patients gain more from sacituzumab govitecan).
There is a positive interaction between her2_positive and treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: No interaction (beta=-0.038, p=0.63). Sacituzumab does not differentially benefit HER2-low patients in this cohort.
No interaction (beta=+0.10, p=0.29).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between postmenopausal status and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months (after adjusting for ER status): postmenopausal patients have differential benefit from palbociclib.
There is a three-way interaction between postmenopausal, er_positive, and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Modest negative postmenopausal x palbociclib interaction (beta=-0.14, p=0.003). Postmenopausal patients have slightly less palbociclib benefit; magnitude small.
Three-way interaction marginal (beta=-0.16, p=0.10, NS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a comprehensive multivariable OLS model of pfs_months that includes age, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, LDH, Ki-67, ER, HER2, HER2-low, PIK3CA, all six treatments, the er_positive x treatment_palbociclib and pik3ca_mutation x treatment_palbociclib interactions, and TNBC, the er_positive x treatment_palbociclib interaction remains a strong positive predictor and the pik3ca_mutation x treatment_palbociclib interaction remains a strong negative predictor.
The full multivariable model explains a large portion of variance in pfs_months (R^2 > 0.8).

analyses.result_summary: treatment_palbociclib:er_positive beta = +1.548 (t=+74.4, p<1e-300); treatment_palbociclib:pik3ca_mutation beta = -1.655 (t=-82.6, p<1e-300). Both effects are robust and clinically meaningful. Other treatment main effects collapse to ~0 after adjustment, confirming the only treatment with detectable PFS effect is palbociclib in the ER+/PIK3CA-WT subgroup.

Full model R^2 = 0.834 (adj R^2 = 0.834), driven by age, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, ER+, HER2+, PIK3CA, Ki-67, and the palbociclib predictive interactions. Strong explanatory power.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the ER+ subgroup, there is a strong negative interaction between pik3ca_mutation and treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months: PIK3CA-WT/ER+ patients gain ~2.4 months from palbociclib while PIK3CA-MUT/ER+ patients gain essentially 0.
Within the ER+ subgroup, in PIK3CA-WT patients (the reference category), treatment_palbociclib has a large positive effect on pfs_months (~+2.4 months).

analyses.code: sub = df[df.er_positive==1]; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ pik3ca_mutation * treatment_palbociclib', sub).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+: pik3ca_mutation:treatment_palbociclib interaction beta=-2.34, p<1e-300. Within ER+/PIK3CA-WT, palbociclib raises mean PFS from 4.30 (n=14754) to 6.68 (n=8034). Within ER+/PIK3CA-MUT, palbociclib has essentially no effect (4.33 vs 4.36).
Within ER+: treatment_palbociclib main coefficient (in PIK3CA-WT reference) = +2.376 months (p<1e-300). Within ER+ disease, palbociclib's PFS gain is essentially confined to PIK3CA-WT tumors.
